path.name: Workforce Systems Programming
path.description: A live workforce systems course where developers and technical builders use JavaScript and AI to design a Screeps colony, turn game mechanics into architecture, and prove the system in capture-the-flag competition.
path.targetAudience: High school programmers, college CS students, junior software engineers, career switchers, and technical builders who are comfortable reading and modifying code and want to become more sophisticated systems thinkers.

programs.name: AI Systems Programming Lab
programs.description: A four-week live cohort where developers and technical builders use JavaScript and Codex to design a Screeps colony that gathers resources, makes decisions, recovers from failures, and battles other colonies in capture-the-flag.
programs.learningOutcomes: Read a live software environment, model available components, design colony roles, manage state, automate resource decisions, debug live behavior, use Git and Codex responsibly, prepare tournament strategy, and explain why the system behaves the way it does.
programs.projectSummary: A Screeps colony system you design that can harvest energy, spawn creeps, assign roles, persist memory, recover from failures, respond to hostile pressure, adapt to resource constraints, and compete under capture-the-flag rules.
programs.portfolioArtifact: Your Git repository with Screeps bot code, commits, README notes, screenshots or match clips, architecture notes, tournament-week strategy notes, and a final reflection on what happened when your system battled other colonies.
programs.liveSchedule: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 6:30 PM-8:30 PM Eastern
programs.cohortNote: New cohorts run every so often. The next cohort opens Monday, August 3, 2026. Live sessions begin Tuesday, August 4, 2026. Phase 2 league rollout follows after cohort tournaments are running.

sessions.name: Session 1: Screeps Environment and System Map
sessions.objectives: Read Screeps as a programmable environment: rooms, creeps, spawns, sources, controllers, terrain, game ticks, state, and available actions.
sessions.liveActivity: Set up the workspace, inspect the Screeps API surface, map the first room, and identify the signals a colony needs before it can make useful decisions.
sessions.homework: Create an environment map that labels components, constraints, signals, and decisions the colony needs to understand.

sessions.name: Session 2: Component Architecture and Role Design
sessions.objectives: Turn Screeps objects into a colony architecture with roles, behaviors, state, and priority rules.
sessions.liveActivity: Design harvester, upgrader, builder, and logistics behaviors; connect them to Memory; and decide how the colony should route work each tick.
sessions.homework: Implement or revise two role components and document their inputs, outputs, assumptions, and failure cases.

sessions.name: Session 3: Decisions, State, and Colony Automation
sessions.objectives: Build decision loops that use state, resource signals, spawn capacity, and room priorities to keep the colony moving.
sessions.liveActivity: Create spawn automation, role balancing, task switching, and fallback behavior for stuck or idle creeps.
sessions.homework: Add one automation loop and document its trigger, expected effect, and failure mode.

sessions.name: Session 4: APIs, Interfaces, and Debugging
sessions.objectives: Use the Screeps API deliberately by reading object contracts, method calls, return values, errors, and live room behavior.
sessions.liveActivity: Inspect Game, Room, Creep, StructureSpawn, Source, Controller, Store, and Memory usage; debug broken behavior from logs and visible colony state.
sessions.homework: Write an API note for one Screeps object or method and use it to fix or improve one colony behavior.

sessions.name: Session 5: AI Build Partner and Feature Planning
sessions.objectives: Use Codex as an architecture and implementation partner without surrendering judgment.
sessions.liveActivity: Choose a feature path, prompt Codex with API-aware context, inspect generated code, test in Screeps, review diffs, and commit only explainable changes.
sessions.homework: Use Codex to implement one approved improvement and write what changed, what was accepted, and what was rejected.

sessions.name: Session 6: Colony Expansion Projects
sessions.objectives: Build a project-grade colony subsystem that deepens architecture, API fluency, and tournament readiness.
sessions.liveActivity: Select and build one colony project: smarter spawn planner, role scheduler, pathing/cost-matrix helper, repair-and-build planner, tower/defense controller, or energy logistics dashboard.
sessions.homework: Finish the project branch, update the README with architecture notes, and identify how the subsystem changes tournament strategy.

sessions.name: Session 7: Tournament Week: Battle Branch and CTF Prep
sessions.objectives: Turn the colony architecture into a battle-ready branch for AutoNateAI capture-the-flag rules.
sessions.liveActivity: Review mechanics, identify attack and defense priorities, tune the tournament branch, and decide what the bot should protect, chase, ignore, or optimize.
sessions.homework: Finish the tournament branch and write a short strategy note explaining how your colony plans to win.

sessions.name: Session 8: Tournament Week: Capture-the-Flag Battles
sessions.objectives: Battle colonies head-to-head in AutoNateAI capture-the-flag, explain the architecture, review tradeoffs, and identify the next version for league-style improvement.
sessions.liveActivity: Participants submit tournament branches, run Screeps capture-the-flag matches, watch code-driven colony strategies collide, and analyze how mechanics, state, automation, and architecture shaped the outcome.
sessions.homework: Submit final repo link, Screeps match screenshots or clips, architecture notes, tournament reflection, and next-build plan.

offerings.name: AI Systems Programming Lab - August Cohort
offerings.meetingFrequency: 4 weeks, Tuesdays and Thursdays, 6:30 PM-8:30 PM Eastern

product.title: AutoNateAI Tournament Day
product.cta: The final week is AutoNateAI tournament week: builders tune battle branches, then play capture-the-flag inside Screeps where every move, defense, and resource decision comes from code.

season.name: August Cohort Tournament Day
season.winCondition: Participants submit battle-ready Screeps bot branches to the tournament server. Colonies go head-to-head under capture-the-flag rules while builders explain how their environment reading, component design, Git history, automation decisions, and strategy shaped the match.
